filters.q: has:attachment subject:(application OR CV OR resume OR apply) -label:hr-processed

nodes.name: Gmail Trigger — New Application Email1
nodes.notes: Polls Gmail every minute. Matches emails with attachments whose subject contains 'application', 'CV', 'resume', or 'apply'. Skips already-processed emails via the 'hr-processed' label. downloadAttachments:true is critical — it makes n8n download attachment binary data into the item.

nodes.name: Set Application Metadata1
nodes.notes: Code node (not Set node) — required because $binary is inaccessible in Set nodes. Extracts email address, sender name, subject, and attachment metadata from all known Gmail Trigger output paths with fallbacks. Passes $binary through to downstream extraction nodes.

nodes.name: Validate: Has Email + Attachment1
nodes.notes: Reads attachment_count and applicant_email from the upstream Code node output. Both fields are now correctly populated from $json and $binary.

assignments.name: skipped_reason
assignments.value: No valid email address or no CV attachment found

nodes.name: Log Skipped Email1
nodes.notes: Dead-end branch — invalid emails are logged and workflow stops here for that item.

nodes.name: Check Duplicate in Google Sheets1
nodes.notes: Looks up email + role_id combination in the Candidates sheet. If a row is found, it's a duplicate. continueOnFail=true means 'no row found' errors pass through.

nodes.name: Is Duplicate Application?1
nodes.notes: If Google Sheets returned a row (email field is populated), this is a duplicate. True = duplicate found → stop. False = no existing record → proceed.

assignments.name: duplicate_reason
assignments.value: Application already exists for this email and role

nodes.name: Log Duplicate — Stop1
nodes.notes: Duplicate applications are logged and silently dropped — no email sent to avoid confusion.

nodes.name: Detect CV Attachment Type1
nodes.notes: Inspects all binary attachments, ranks by file type priority (PDF > DOCX > DOC > TXT), and selects the best CV candidate. Sets extraction_strategy for the branching router below.

nodes.name: Route by File Type1
nodes.notes: Splits into PDF, DOCX, TXT, and ERROR branches. Each file type gets a dedicated extraction node.

nodes.name: Extract Text from PDF1
nodes.notes: Uses n8n's native PDF extractor. Reads binary data from the cv_binary_key attachment. Handles multi-page CVs up to 20 pages. Output is in $json.text

nodes.name: Extract Text from TXT1
nodes.notes: Plain text extraction for .txt CVs. Rare but handled.

assignments.name: cv_extraction_error_msg
assignments.value: ={{ $json.cv_extraction_error_msg || 'Unsupported file type — cannot extract CV text' }}

nodes.name: Handle Extraction Error1
nodes.notes: Catches unsupported file types or detection failures. Workflow stops here for this item.

nodes.name: Merge Extracted CV Text1
nodes.notes: Recombines PDF, DOCX, and TXT branches back into a single stream before text normalization.

nodes.name: Normalize CV Text1
nodes.notes: Critical quality layer: strips OCR artifacts, control characters, excessive whitespace. Falls back to email body text if extracted content is too short (catches image-based PDFs). Computes word/char count and assigns a quality tier.

parameters.subject: Re: Your Application — CV Could Not Be Processed
parameters.message:
=

Dear Applicant,

Thank you for applying. Unfortunately, we were unable to extract text from your attached CV ({{ $json.cv_filename }}).

This usually happens when the CV is a scanned image PDF. Please resend your application with a text-searchable PDF or a .docx file.

We apologize for the inconvenience and look forward to receiving your updated application.

Best regards,
The Hiring Team

nodes.name: Email Candidate: Resubmit CV1
nodes.notes: Automatically emails the candidate asking them to resubmit a text-based CV. Handles scanned/image PDFs gracefully.

parameters.text:
=You are a CV parsing engine. Extract structured information from the CV text below.

IMPORTANT FAIRNESS RULES — STRICTLY ENFORCED:
- Do NOT extract or return: name, gender, age, date of birth, ethnicity, nationality, marital status, photo description, home address, religion, or any protected characteristic
- Extract ONLY: skills, education credentials, work experience (titles + durations + responsibilities), years of experience, certifications, and languages spoken

Return your answer as a JSON object with this exact schema:
{
  "skills": ["skill1", "skill2"],
  "education": [{"degree": "", "field": "", "institution": "", "year": ""}],
  "experience": [{"title": "", "company": "REDACTED", "duration_months": 0, "responsibilities": ["..."], "technologies_used": ["..."]}],
  "total_years_experience": 0.0,
  "certifications": ["cert1"],
  "languages": ["English"],
  "professional_summary": "",
  "notable_achievements": ["achievement1"]
}

For duration_months: calculate from dates if present, or estimate from context (e.g. '2 years' = 24).
For total_years_experience: sum all non-overlapping professional experience.

CV TEXT:
{{ $json.cv_raw_text }}

Email body context (use only if CV text is ambiguous):
{{ $json.email_body_text }}

options.systemMessage:
You are a precise, bias-free CV parsing assistant. You must respond with ONLY a valid JSON object.
Do NOT include any text before or after the JSON.
Do NOT wrap it in markdown code blocks or backticks.
Do NOT add any explanation, notes, or commentary.
Your entire response must begin with an opening curly brace and end with a closing curly brace. You never include personally identifying information beyond what is in the schema. You are thorough and extract all technical skills mentioned anywhere in the CV, including from job responsibilities and project descriptions.

nodes.name: AI Agent — Parse CV (Bias-Free)1
nodes.notes: Native n8n AI Agent node. Replaces the raw OpenAI HTTP request. Uses conversationalAgent mode with a strict bias-free system prompt. Connected to an OpenAI Chat Model sub-node below.

options.systemMessage: You are a fair, transparent HR assessment assistant. You explain scoring decisions in plain language. You only reference qualifications and skills — never personal characteristics. You return only valid JSON.

nodes.name: AI Agent — Explainability Engine1
nodes.notes: Native AI Agent node replacing the OpenAI HTTP request for explainability. Uses gpt-4o-mini for speed and cost efficiency.

options.systemMessage: You are an expert technical interviewer. You generate insightful, role-specific interview questions. You base questions entirely on professional qualifications and skills — never on personal characteristics. You return only valid JSON.

nodes.name: AI Agent — Generate Interview Questions1
nodes.notes: Native AI Agent node replacing the OpenAI HTTP request for question generation. Uses gpt-4o for highest quality questions.

nodes.name: Label Email as HR-Processed1
nodes.notes: Adds 'hr-processed' Gmail label so the trigger filter skips it next poll. Create this label in Gmail first. Replace Label_HRProcessed with the actual label ID from your Gmail account.

parameters.subject: ={{ 'Interview Invitation — ' + $('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.jd_title }}
parameters.message:
=

Interview Invitation

Dear Applicant,

Thank you for your interest in the {{ $('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.jd_title }} position. After reviewing your application, we are delighted to invite you to the next stage of our hiring process.

Book Your Interview Slot

Please use the link below to select a time that works best for you:

Choose Interview Time

Interview Format: 45–60 minutes (technical + experience discussion)

Role: {{ $('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.jd_title }}

Application ID: {{ $('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.application_id }}

If you have any questions, please reply directly to this email.

We look forward to speaking with you!

Best regards,
The Hiring Team

additionalFields.description:
={{ 
  'Application ID: ' + $('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.application_id + 
  '
Role: ' + $('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.jd_title + 
  '
Score: ' + $('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.composite_score + '/100 (' + $('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.fit_tier + ')' +
  '
CV File: ' + $('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.cv_filename + 
  '

--- AI ASSESSMENT ---
' + 
  ($('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.overall_summary || '') + 
  '

Strengths: ' + ($('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.strengths || []).join(', ') + 
  '
Gaps to probe: ' + ($('Store Candidate → Google Sheets1').item.json.missing_required || []).join(', ') + 
  '

--- INTERVIEW QUESTIONS ---

TECHNICAL:
' + 
  ($('AI Agent — Generate Interview Questions1').item.json.output || '')
}}

nodes.name: Create Google Calendar Event1
nodes.notes: Calendar description includes full interview question set so interviewers are always prepared.

nodes.name: Lookup JD from Google Sheets1
nodes.notes: Looks up the Job Descriptions sheet by role_id. Sheet columns: role_id, jd_title, required_skills (comma-sep), preferred_skills (comma-sep), min_years, preferred_years, required_education, preferred_certifications (comma-sep), responsibilities (pipe-sep), skills_weight, experience_weight, education_weight. Add a new row per role — no workflow editing needed.
